title: dactylis glomerata subsp. marina
intro: Dactylis glomerata subsp. marina, also known as coastal cock's-foot, is a subspecies of the perennial grass Dactylis glomerata. This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in reducing inflammation and treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Dactylis glomerata subsp. marina include its potential to soothe digestive problems, reduce fever, and alleviate menstrual cramps, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Coastal cock's-foot can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
